MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-07-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Evelyn Vitalich 415-515-7827 personal representative for Plaintiff. (in-person)
Sarah Gates, Esq. 949-788-1790 Pleis Sitar McGRath Gates & Ajello for Defendant DEFENDANT GOLDEN CALIFORNIA HEALTHCARE, LLC et al.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court states it did not receive a contest email from the Plaintiff.
Ms. Vitalich argues she is sick and the pcp is busy. The Court announces it did not receive any expert declaration. Ms. Gates responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruilng as follows:

DEFENDANT GOLDEN CALIFORNIA HEALTHCARE, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication

Defendant GOLDEN CALIFORNIA HEALTHCARE, LLC's motion for summary judgment is granted.

"When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence." (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.)

Here, the expert declaration of Dr. Luxenberg establishes that defendant neither breached the standard of care nor was a substantial factor in causing decedent's injury/death. Defendant maintained its burden of production. (CCP 437c(p)(2).) In opposition, plaintiff fails to proffer any expert testimony that creates a triable issue of fact regarding breach or causation. Defendant is therefore entitled to summary judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2022 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-19-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Sep-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EVELYN VITALICH's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Evelyn Vitalich's motion to set aside dismissal is granted. California courts strongly favor disposing of cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) Here, the motion was filed less than four months after entry of default.

"[C]ompliance with the statutory procedures for service of process is essential to establish personal jurisdiction. Under [Code of Civil Procedure] section 473, subdivision (d), the court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service." (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544.)

Plaintiff declares that she was never personally served written discovery motions and subsequent motions to compel and for terminating sanctions in this action - that at the time of service she lived at a different address than was supplied by her former attorney in the order relieving him of counsel. (Vitalich Decl. at 3.) Plaintiff asserts in her former attorney's moving papers the address is 101 A street, Unit 22, Colma, CA, instead of Unit 212. (Geerhart Decl., Exh. 1.) Defendant declares Plaintiff was served via email December 3, 2021 and Defendants called Plaintiff on December 2, 2021. (Gates Decl.) However, Defendant does not provide any admissible evidence that controverts Plaintiffs' declaration that service failed to result in actual notice. Finally, Plaintiff's calls into question the veracity of these statements, and "[i]t is the policy of the law to favor, whenever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . Therefore, when a party in default moves promptly to seek relief, very slight evidence is required to justify a trial court's order setting aside a default. (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal.3d 474, 478.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SARAH P. GATES, Esq. of Pleiss Sitar McGrath Hunter & Hallack (Tel: 949-788-1790) for defendants Golden California Healthcare, LLC dba Central Gardens Post Acute and Joel Malbalot, LVN;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GOLDEN CALIFORNIA HEALTHCARE, LLC's Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

Defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS HAVE FILED NO RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. BASED UPON THE PAPERS SUBMITTED IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH PRIOR ORDERS OF THIS COURT WHICHH WOULD JUSTIFY THE COURT AT THIS TIME TO ENTER AN ORDER DISMISSING PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT WITH PREJUDICE. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL GIVE PLAINTIFFS THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER TO SERVE DEFENDANT, WITHOUT OBJECTION, FULL AND COMPLETE VERIFIED, CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER, THE COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION OF THIS MATTER AND WILL AT THAT TIME ENTER AN ORDER DISMISSING PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT WITH PREJUDICE.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by defense counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
